title: ERC6551 relay proxy fallback with lazy default-implementation initialization for Etherscan compatibility
explanation: Three optimizations: (1) `mstore(0x40, returndatasize())` — at the start of fallback, returndatasize is 0, so this is a free way to write 0 to the free memory pointer location, but it tricks the compiler into emitting `3d604052` (RETURNDATASIZE PUSH1 0x40 MSTORE = 4 bytes) instead of the standard `6080604052` (PUSH1 0x80 PUSH1 0x40 MSTORE = 5 bytes), shaving 1 byte off every fallback dispatch. (2) Lazy initialization: the impl slot is only written on calldatasize == 0 (sending 0 ETH triggers the fallback with no calldata). This means staticcalls to view functions (which always have a 4-byte selector at minimum) never accidentally write to the impl slot, while still allowing Etherscan's 'Is this a proxy?' detection to work after a single 0-ETH ping. (3) returndatasize() is reused as a literal 0 for input/output offsets and length, saving the explicit PUSH1 0x00 opcodes. codesize() serves the same role for the call output position.
when_to_apply: ERC6551 token-bound accounts that need to be upgradeable via a relay proxy, with Etherscan proxy detection support
when_not_to_apply: Non-upgradeable ERC6551 accounts, contexts where the bytecode-size optimization is irrelevant
